stem: A manufacturing quality inspection uses 4 parallel inspectors for redundancy. The defect caught requires at least one inspector to function. inspector 1 has a reliability of 91%. inspector 2 has a reliability of 90%. inspector 3 has a reliability between 74% and 84% (uncertain due to limited operational data). inspector 4 has a reliability of 76%. Additionally, there is a common-cause failure probability (e.g., power outage, shared software bug) that could disable all inspectors simultaneously, estimated between 3.3% and 7.0%. What is the overall probability that the defect caught?

ground_truth.answer: P(defect caught) ranges from 0.9294 to 0.9667. Point estimate: 0.9475.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 4 inspectors fail simultaneously).
With common-cause failure (3.3%-7.0%): P(works) = (1-P_cc) × P(independent works). Range: [0.9294, 0.9667].
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
Ignoring common-cause failures that can disable all paths simultaneously
